summary: Comprehensive multi-branch stress test across Main Branch Downtown, IPIL Branch, and Sampoli Branch. 61/61 backend pytest tests passed. All major features tested: sales (cash+credit), POs (create+receive+pay), inventory management, customer creation, fund setup, repack creation, expenses, AR payments, close wizard (steps 1-8 navigable), offline sync, low stock alert, supplier payables. One CRITICAL backend bug found: payment_method case mismatch causing cash_sales=₱0 in Z-reports.

sampoli_branch.platinum_repack: R PLATINUM SMALL (12/case) created → is_repack=true, units_per_parent=12 ✓

ar_balances.janmark: Increased by ₱3,200 from PLATINUM credit sale ✓

critical.issue: CRITICAL payment_method case mismatch: frontend UnifiedSalesPage.js line 584 sends 'Cash' (capital C), daily_operations.py queries with lowercase 'cash'. Result: all cash sales show as ₱0 in Z-report/daily log in real production usage.
critical.root_cause: frontend/src/pages/UnifiedSalesPage.js line 584: payment_method: actualPaymentType === 'cash' ? 'Cash' : 'Credit' sends capital C. Backend daily_ops.py queries: {'payment_method': 'cash'} expects lowercase.
critical.fix: Change UnifiedSalesPage.js line 584 to use lowercase 'cash' and 'credit', OR fix daily_operations.py queries to use case-insensitive $regex match. Also normalize default in sales.py from 'Cash' to 'cash'.

minor.issue: Wizard shows 'All products adequately stocked' for IPIL but dashboard shows 6 low stock items - discrepancy between dashboard count and wizard alert. Low stock thresholds may differ.
Close wizard step 7 Verify button disabled when steps 5&6 not completed - correct behavior but navigation jumps to step 7 without completing prerequisites shows misleading state.

integration_issues.flow: Close Wizard IPIL Step 1 Sales Log
integration_issues.issue: Shows Cash Sales: ₱0 even after cash sales were made today. Root cause is the payment_method case bug (same as backend critical issue). Affects all branches.

issues: Shows 1 cash sale for Main Branch correctly when lowercase 'cash' used. All other branches show 0 due to frontend bug.

ipil_branch_2026_02_21.step7_close_sign_off: Z-Report preview shows all fields. Manager PIN input present. Verify button disabled until steps 5&6 complete (correct behavior).
ipil_branch_2026_02_21.supplier_payables_modal: Shows PILMICO POs as DUE SOON (3 days) ✓
ipil_branch_2026_02_21.low_stock_modal: Shows 'All products adequately stocked' (discrepancy with dashboard's 6 items)
ipil_branch_2026_02_21.offline_indicator: Online → Ready for offline. Synced: 14 products, 10 customers, 4 stock records ✓

offline_sync_verification.indicator: Shows 'Ready for offline', 'Synced just now · IPIL BRANCH', '14 products · 10 customers · 4 stock records'
offline_sync_verification.notes: Offline sync working correctly. During branch switch, sync progress shown (92% - 'Saving price schemes & branch...')

supplier_payables_check.pilmico_po: Shows as DUE SOON (3 days, 2026-02-24) with balance ₱5,000 after partial payment ✓
supplier_payables_check.note: Both PILMICO POs from two test runs show in payables. URGENT label appears as 'DUE SOON' badge in red/orange

manager_pin_test.ui_verification: PIN input present in Step 7 but Verify button disabled until prerequisites met

action_items: CRITICAL FIX: UnifiedSalesPage.js line 584 - change 'Cash' to 'cash' and 'Credit' to 'credit' (lowercase). Also fix backend/utils/helpers.py log_sale_items to normalize payment_method.lower(). This makes all Z-reports show cash_sales correctly.
Verify Low Stock Alert discrepancy: dashboard shows 6 low stock items for IPIL but /api/low-stock-alert returns 0. Check threshold logic between dashboard count and API.
Consider making daily_operations.py queries case-insensitive for payment_method as a defensive fix: use $regex instead of exact match.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL: frontend/src/pages/UnifiedSalesPage.js line 584: payment_method sends 'Cash'/'Credit' (capitalized) but backend daily_operations.py queries for lowercase 'cash'/'credit'. This breaks all Z-reports and daily logs in production.
backend/utils/helpers.py log_sale_items should normalize payment_method to lowercase before storing in sales_log.
backend/routes/daily_operations.py lines with payment_method filter should use case-insensitive matching as defensive coding.
Safe deposits are doubling on each test run (not idempotent) - ₱60,000 IPIL safe = 2 × ₱30,000 deposits. Not a bug per se but test cleanup needed.

success_rate.frontend: 90% (close wizard, daily ops, offline sync, supplier payables all work; cash sales reporting broken due to payment_method case bug)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All transactions created successfully and verified. CRITICAL BUG: payment_method case sensitivity (UnifiedSalesPage.js line 584 sends 'Cash' but daily_ops.py filters for 'cash'). After fix, re-verify: (1) Z-report shows correct cash_sales > 0, (2) daily_log shows cash entries, (3) close wizard step 1 shows cash sales. Test credentials: owner/521325. All branch IDs and customer/product IDs in shared_state. Fund wallets now higher than initial due to multiple test runs (IPIL safe=₱60k, Sampoli safe=₱40k). 2 PILMICO POs created (from 2 test runs), both partial with ₱5,000 balance each.

rca_of_issue.bug: Cash sales showing ₱0 in Z-report/daily close preview
rca_of_issue.root_cause: Case mismatch: frontend sends payment_method='Cash' but backend queries payment_method='cash'
rca_of_issue.reproduction: Create a sale via frontend UnifiedSalesPage. Check daily close preview or daily log. cash_sales will be ₱0 despite transactions existing.
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Fix UnifiedSalesPage.js line 584: change 'Cash' to 'cash' and 'Credit' to 'credit'. OR fix daily_operations.py to use case-insensitive regex. Recommend both.
